```{r simulation iteration wrapper function, eval=false, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
    )

    ## 2.5 Extract AR + cross-lags per model --------------------------------

    lag_raw  <- extract_lagged_parameters(fit_clpm_raw, T, "clpm")
    lag_ric  <- extract_lagged_parameters(fit_ric,       T, "riclpm")
    lag_dpm0 <- extract_lagged_parameters(fit_dpm0,      T, "dpm")
    lag_adj  <- extract_lagged_parameters(fit_adj,       T, "clpm")
    lag_lbca <- extract_lagged_parameters(fit_lbca,      T, "clpm")

    fail_CLPM      <- is.null(fit_clpm_raw)
    fail_RI_CLPM   <- is.null(fit_ric)
    fail_DPM       <- is.null(fit_dpm0)
    fail_CLPM_Adj  <- is.null(fit_adj)
    fail_CLPM_LBCA <- is.null(fit_lbca)

    ## 2.6 Assemble long-format output (T rows, this scenario) --------------

    out_list[[j]] <- data.frame(
      run        = rep(rep_id, T),
      occasion   = 1:T,
      scenario   = pretty_scen(scen),

      beta       = I(beta_full),
      beta_X     = I(beta_X),
      beta_Y     = I(beta_Y),

      true_cross = cross,
      true_auto  = ar,

      # cross-lag X->Y (NA at t = 1)
      est_RI_CLPM   = c(NA, lag_ric$xy),
      est_DPM       = c(NA, lag_dpm0$xy),
      est_CLPM      = c(NA, lag_raw$xy),
      est_CLPM_LBCA = c(NA, lag_lbca$xy),
      est_CLPM_Adj  = c(NA, lag_adj$xy),

      # AR for X (NA at t = 1)
      estA_RI_CLPM   = c(NA, lag_ric$ar_x),
      estA_DPM       = c(NA, lag_dpm0$ar_x),
      estA_CLPM      = c(NA, lag_raw$ar_x),
      estA_CLPM_LBCA = c(NA, lag_lbca$ar_x),
      estA_CLPM_Adj  = c(NA, lag_adj$ar_x),

      fail_CLPM       = fail_CLPM,
      fail_RI_CLPM    = fail_RI_CLPM,
      fail_DPM        = fail_DPM,
      fail_CLPM_LBCA  = fail_CLPM_LBCA,
      fail_CLPM_Adj   = fail_CLPM_Adj,

      is_na_run = as.integer(
        all(is.na(c(lag_raw$xy,
                    lag_ric$xy,
                    lag_dpm0$xy,
                    lag_adj$xy,
                    lag_lbca$xy)))
      )
    )
  }

  # bind scenarios for this run
  dplyr::bind_rows(out_list)
}


```

and then a function to iteratively call this wrapper for multiple simulation repetitions:

```{r multiple replication wrapper function, eval=false, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}

############################################################
##  MAIN SIMULATION: STUDY 1 (LINEAR ONLY, LONG OUTPUT)
############################################################

run_simulation_study1 <- function(
  reps      = 200,
  N         = 1000,
  T         = 5,
  k         = 3,
  R2_1      = 0.20,
  scenarios = c("constant","linear","sinusoidal","stepwise","random_walk"),
  target_sd = 0.10,
  ar        = 0.25,
  cross     = 0.10,
  rho       = 0.15,
  cores     = max(1, parallel::detectCores() - 1),
  base_seed = 12345
){

  # match/validate scenario names (allows subsets if you want)
  scenarios <- match.arg(scenarios,
                         choices = c("constant","linear","sinusoidal",
                                     "stepwise","random_walk"),
                         several.ok = TRUE)

  start_time <- Sys.time()

  ## 1. Build model strings once (on the master) -----------------------------

  model_clpm          <- build_clpm(T)
  model_riclpm        <- build_riclpm(T)
  model_dpm           <- build_dpm(T)
  model_clpm_with_Cs  <- build_clpm_with_Cs(T, k)

  tasks <- 1:reps

  ## 2. Start cluster --------------------------------------------------------

  cl <- parallel::makeCluster(cores)

  ## 3. Export everything needed to workers ---------------------------------

  parallel::clusterExport(
    cl,
    c(
      "sample_B_linear",
      "generate_B_trajectory",
      "simulate_panel_data",

      "build_clpm",
      "build_riclpm",
      "build_dpm",
      "build_clpm_with_Cs",

      "fit_clpm",
      "fit_riclpm",
      "fit_dpm",
      "fit_clpm_with_Cs",
      "fit_clpm_resid",

      "residualise_panel_linearC",
      "extract_lagged_parameters",
      "run_one_rep_study1",

      # fixed scalar arguments:
      "N","T","k","R2_1","scenarios","target_sd",
      "ar","cross","rho",
      "model_clpm","model_riclpm","model_dpm","model_clpm_with_Cs",
      "base_seed"
    ),
    envir = environment()
  )

  ## 4. Load packages on workers --------------------------------------------

  parallel::clusterEvalQ(cl, {
    library(mvtnorm)
    library(lavaan)
  })

  ## 5. Parallel loop with pbapply progress bar -----------------------------

  res_list <- pbapply::pblapply(
    X  = tasks,
    cl = cl,
    FUN = function(rid) {
      run_one_rep_study1(
        rep_id           = rid,
        N                = N,
        T                = T,
        k                = k,
        R2_1             = R2_1,
        scenarios        = scenarios,
        target_sd        = target_sd,
        ar               = ar,
        cross            = cross,
        rho              = rho,
        model_clpm       = model_clpm,
        model_riclpm     = model_riclpm,
        model_dpm        = model_dpm,
        model_clpm_with_Cs = model_clpm_with_Cs,
        base_seed        = base_seed
      )
    }
  )

  parallel::stopCluster(cl)

  raw_df  <- dplyr::bind_rows(res_list)
  runtime <- Sys.time() - start_time

  attr(raw_df, "runtime") <- runtime
  raw_df
}


```

## 2.8 Running a simulation function

Even though we have defined all code above, debugging that is a huge pain in the ass (mainly caused by the parralell exectution). The true code that generates the results is stored in a script. Do note that it works the same. 

```{r run simulation example}

# get the functions
source("C:/Users/Admin/Desktop/UU/Thesis umbrella/Thesis/Code/Qmd_Rmd_R_Pyth/Sim Studies/Sim_Study_1_forqmd.R")

# create the output
out_long <- run_simulation_study1(
  reps      = 250,
  N         = 1000,
  T         = 5,
  k         = 3,
  R2_1      = 0.25,
  target_sd = 0.15,
  ar        = 0.2,
  cross     = 0.10,
  rho       = 0.15
)

# save the output as a dataframe
saveRDS(
  out_long,
  file = "C:/Users/Admin/Desktop/UU/Thesis umbrella/Thesis/Code/Data/out_long_study1.rds"
)

```

## 2.9 Making the plots

Now we make the plots we discussed earlier:

* Plot 1: 

```{r }

# read the rds file
df <- readRDS("C:/Users/Admin/Desktop/UU/Thesis umbrella/Thesis/Code/Data/out_long_study1.rds")
str(df)


# colours
blue_magenta <- c(
  "#3B4CC0",  
  "#5E60CE",
  "#7A70D1",
  "#9775D3",
  "#B67BD2",
  "#D084CF",
  "#E28FC4",
  "#F29BC1",
  "#FF9ACD"
)

method_colors <- c(
  "RI_CLPM"   = "#3B4CC0",
  "DPM"       = "#5E60CE",
  "CLPM"      = "#7A70D1",
  "CLPM_LBCA" = "#B67BD2",
  "CLPM_Adj"  = "#EE3A8C"
)

# extend colours to number of runs
n_runs <- length(unique(df$run))
blue_magenta_extended <- rep(blue_magenta, length.out = n_runs)
plot_beta_X <- df %>%
  mutate(beta_X = map_dbl(beta_X, ~ .x)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = occasion, y = beta_X,
             group = run, color = factor(run))) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.9) +
  facet_wrap(~ scenario) +
  scale_color_manual(values = blue_magenta_extended) +
  guides(color = "none") +
  labs(x = "Occasion", y = "Effect of Confounder on X") +
  theme_minimal()

plot_beta_X

plot_beta_Y <- df %>%
  mutate(beta_Y = map_dbl(beta_Y, ~ .x)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = occasion, y = beta_Y,
             group = run, color = factor(run))) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.9) +
  facet_wrap(~ scenario) +
  scale_color_manual(values = blue_magenta_extended) +
  guides(color = "none") +
  labs(x = "Occasion", y = "Effect of Confounder on Y") +
  theme_minimal()

plot_beta_Y

plot_R2_X <- df %>%
  mutate(beta_X = map_dbl(beta_X, ~ .x),
         R2_X = beta_X^2) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = occasion, y = R2_X,
             group = run, color = factor(run))) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.9) +
  facet_wrap(~ scenario) +
  scale_color_manual(values = blue_magenta_extended) +
  guides(color = "none") +
  labs(x = "Occasion", y = expression(R^2 ~ "(confounder → X)")) +
  theme_minimal()

plot_R2_X

plot_bias_cross <- df %>%
  mutate(
    rb_RI_CLPM   = abs(est_RI_CLPM   - true_cross) / true_cross,
    rb_DPM       = abs(est_DPM       - true_cross) / true_cross,
    rb_CLPM      = abs(est_CLPM      - true_cross) / true_cross,
    rb_CLPM_LBCA = abs(est_CLPM_LBCA - true_cross) / true_cross,
    rb_CLPM_Adj  = abs(est_CLPM_Adj  - true_cross) / true_cross
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    starts_with("rb_"),
    names_to = "method",
    values_to = "rel_bias"
  ) %>%
  mutate(method = gsub("rb_", "", method),
         method = factor(method, levels = names(method_colors))) %>%
  group_by(scenario, method, occasion) %>%
  summarise(mean_bias = mean(rel_bias, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = occasion, y = mean_bias,
             color = method, group = method)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_grid(method ~ scenario) +
  scale_color_manual(values = method_colors) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Occasion", y = "Mean Relative Bias (Cross-Lagged)")

plot_bias_cross

plot_se_cross <- df %>%
  mutate(
    rb_RI_CLPM   = abs(est_RI_CLPM   - true_cross) / true_cross,
    rb_DPM       = abs(est_DPM       - true_cross) / true_cross,
    rb_CLPM      = abs(est_CLPM      - true_cross) / true_cross,
    rb_CLPM_LBCA = abs(est_CLPM_LBCA - true_cross) / true_cross,
    rb_CLPM_Adj  = abs(est_CLPM_Adj  - true_cross) / true_cross
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    starts_with("rb_"),
    names_to = "method",
    values_to = "rel_bias"
  ) %>%
  mutate(method = gsub("rb_", "", method),
         method = factor(method, levels = names(method_colors))) %>%
  group_by(scenario, method, occasion) %>%
  summarise(se_bias = sd(rel_bias, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = occasion, y = se_bias,
             color = method, group = method)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_grid(method ~ scenario) +
  scale_color_manual(values = method_colors) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Occasion", y = "Empirical SE (Cross-Lagged)")

plot_se_cross

plot_summary <- df %>%
  select(scenario, run, starts_with("est_"), true_cross) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    starts_with("est_"),
    names_to = "method",
    values_to = "estimate"
  ) %>%
  mutate(method = gsub("est_", "", method),
         method = factor(method, levels = names(method_colors))) %>%
  group_by(scenario, method) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_est = mean(estimate, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_est   = sd(estimate,   na.rm = TRUE),
    true_val = unique(true_cross),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = method, y = mean_est, color = method)) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_est - sd_est,
                    ymax = mean_est + sd_est),
                width = 0.15, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_hline(aes(yintercept = true_val), linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_grid(scenario ~ .) +
  scale_color_manual(values = method_colors) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 25, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) +
  labs(x = "Method", y = "Estimated Cross-Lagged Effect")

plot_summary

```

# 3. SImulation study 2

## 3.8 Running the simulation function

```{r}

# load the simulation engine
source("C:/Users/Admin/Desktop/UU/Thesis umbrella/Thesis/Code/Qmd_Rmd_R_Pyth/Sim Studies/Sim_Study_2_forqmd.R")

out_long <- run_simulation_study2(
  reps      = 10,
  N         = 1000,
  T         = 5,
  k_lin     = 3,
  ax        = 0.25,
  ay        = 0.25,
  bx        = 0.10,
  by        = 0.10,
  rho       = 0.15,
  R2_1      = 0.25,
  eta_1     = 0.40,
  target_sd = 0.10,
  rw_sd     = 0.10,
  cores     = 6,
  base_seed = 12345
)

# save the results
saveRDS(
  out_long,
  file = "C:/Users/Admin/Desktop/UU/Thesis umbrella/Thesis/Code/Data/out_long_study2.rds"
)

```

## 3.9 Making the plots

```{r}

df <- readRDS("C:/Users/Admin/Desktop/UU/Thesis umbrella/Thesis/Code/Data/out_long_study2.rds")
str(df)

library(tidyverse)




```
